title: Traveling with Kyle - Peru Part 6/7 - Arequipa, Mount Misti, Earthquake

transcript.text: Yeah, so, yeah, earthquake happens, and so I'm like, fuck this, I run outside, and everybody
Runs outside in the middle of the road, and I'm here like, whoa, you almost have to put
Your hand out to keep, the earth is shaking, you're in a freaking earthquake, and you can
Even hear the earth go, and there's all these Indian ladies hugging each other, sobbing
And crying, you know, terrified, and I look over and I see these huge stone blocks fall
Off the buildings and crash onto cars, and then it lasts for maybe 45 seconds, you know,
Without exaggerating, really like 45 seconds, and you just hear like, whoa, am I going to
Die, are these whole buildings going to crash on top of us, you know, because we're all in
The middle of the road, wide, we're like, fuck, and then it stops, and I notice that all the
Dust was just kicked up, and there was just dust everywhere in the sky, and I couldn't
See, you know, you couldn't see more than a couple city blocks from the dust, and you
Look and you couldn't see Misty at all, you could maybe only see the top of Misty from
All the dust that got kicked up, and you know, and I think there was some like, you know, car
Alarms maybe going off, or maybe not, anyway, I'm like, fuck, so I go back to my hotel room,
And I noticed that one of the spires in the church toppled down on top of the plaza, and
There were rubbles strewn all over the place, I didn't see any bodies, so luckily people
Had gotten out of the way, so I was like, snap, and I went and I took another photo of
It from the same place that I was in my hotel room, so I had a before and after picture of
The Arequipa church there, and so I was like, wacky, so then I went up and I bought a, I got
A dinner, and I got a little groundhog, I decided, a groundhog is a delicatessen, and
I saw I get this groundhog, you know, a little dead groundhog cut open, and I eat this meat,
Not very much meat at all, very unfilling, pretty much same meat as chicken, except tougher,
But it's white meat, and kind of reminded me of the time where my Indian friend, Andy,
An Ado friend, he grew up in Alaska, he said he lived in the outback Alaska for three weeks,
And all he had was a BB gun, because he ate groundhogs the whole time with his friend,
But anyways, so I guess, yeah, I guess you can always survive if there's no groundhogs
And rats, but anyways, and that's a delicacy for them, because don't ask me why, but anyway,
And it was expensive, but anyways, then I was like, alright, well let's climb Misty, and most
Of the time people get guides, but I was like, I didn't want to get a guide, so I was an idiot,
I wasn't thinking straightly, because I was high, I should have ridden my bicycle first
On a test ride up the road to the base of Misty, because there was a road, but instead
I rode my bike up at night or something to the edge of town, because I for some reason
Didn't think that there would be a road that would go all the way to Misty, so all night
Long I end up walking 10 miles towards Misty, instead of, well I could have ridden my bike
On the trail, on the road that was there, and not only that, but I was so high that it
Was a moonless night that I, in my brilliance, chose to do it in, and not only that, but I
Forgot to bring a flashlight, because I was so freaking smart, because I was high off my
Ass, not only that, I was so fucking high, I forgot to take my bike helmet off, when I
Locked, when I put, I didn't even lock my bike, I just set my bike next to some trash heap
At the edge of town, I rode through the ghetto, it was a little bit sketchy, to get there,
But not only that, but I filled up all of my, like my only water, my camelback bladder
Thing, with carbonated water, I didn't notice it was normal water, it was carbonated water,
So anyways, I'm like doing this, I can't see anything, literally I can't see anything,
All I know is which direction Misty is, from the outline of it, in the, you know, moonless
Night, and, you know, and I would, walking through freaking thorn bushes, over, through

Balls, and I, and I get to this, like, another one of these ravines, and I sit down, and I'm

I forgot to take my helmet off, I see all these stars, and I was so high that I didn't
